product_brief: Code signing and transparency tool for containers and binaries using Sigstore.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention any Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) algorithms, hybrid modes, or migration plans. It relies on standard certificate authorities (Fulcio) and transparency logs (Rekor) without specifying PQC primitives.
crypto_primitives: Not stated (Document mentions "ephemeral keys", "code signing certificate", and "public key" but does not specify algorithms like ECDSA, Ed25519, or RSA).
key_management_model: Supports "Keyless signing" via Sigstore Fulcio CA, Hardware/KMS signing, encrypted private/public keypairs generated by cosign, and Bring-your-own PKI.
architecture_type: Application-layer code signing tool supporting Keyless (OIDC), KMS/Hardware, and local keypair models.
latest_version: 2.4.1 (mentioned in Dockerfile example); 2.x series is stable; 2.6.x mentioned for troubleshooting.
primary_platforms: Linux, macOS, Docker/Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, Homebrew, Arch, Nix.
target_industries: Software Development, Container Security, DevOps (implied by "containers and binaries", "OCI registry").
signature_schemes: Not stated (Document describes signing workflows but does not list specific signature algorithms).

product_brief: A command-line tool included in the Windows SDK that digitally signs, verifies, removes signatures from, and time stamps files.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention any Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) algorithms, hybrid modes, or PQC migration plans. It explicitly recommends SHA256 over SHA1 but lists no quantum-resistant primitives.
key_management_model: Uses Cryptographic Service Providers (CSP) and private key containers; supports PFX (Personal Information Exchange) files for certificates containing private keys.
architecture_type: Command-line tool utilizing Windows CSPs and certificate stores.
primary_platforms: Windows (part of Windows Software Development Kit, Windows Hardware Lab Kit, Windows Driver Kit, Windows Assessment and Deployment Kit)
target_industries: Enterprise, Software Development, Government (implied by driver signing policies)
signature_schemes: Authenticode signatures using PKCS#7; digest algorithms SHA256 and SHA1.

product_brief: A code signing and software integrity tool offering experimental post-quantum cryptography support for signing and verification.
pqc_capability_description: Experimental support for ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and Falcon (TLS only). Requires enabling on Trust Protection Platform server. Supported for CodeSign Protect clients, libhsm API, and PKCS#11 applications. Higher processing time due to key sizes. Limited certificate formats (DER, PEM/PKCS#8, PKCS#7).
key_management_model: Software-based storage required for PQC environments; supports HSM connectors generally but PQC keys must use "Software" location. Supports PKCS#8 and PKCS#12 formats.
architecture_type: Application-layer with server-side Trust Protection Platform integration
license: Proprietary (subject to active license agreement with Venafi, Inc. and/or CyberArk Ltd.)
primary_platforms: Trust Protection Platform server; CodeSign Protect clients (CLI); libhsm API; PKCS#11 applications
pqc_roadmap_details: Experimental support added to help plan for future PQC migration; standardization approaching. Algorithms: ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, Falcon.
signature_schemes: ML-DSA (Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Standard), SLH-DSA (Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Standard), Falcon (for TLS certificates only)

product_name: RPM Signing (rpm-sign) in Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.1
product_brief: Tool for signing RPM packages with quantum-resistant cryptography using hybrid keys and multiple signatures.
pqc_support: Yes (Technology preview in RHEL 10.1; supports ML-DSA-87+Ed448 and ML-DSA-65+Ed25519)
pqc_capability_description: Technology preview in RHEL 10.1 using Sequoia-PGP tools. Supports hybrid signatures (ML-DSA-87+Ed448, ML-DSA-65+Ed25519) via RPMv6 format allowing multiple signatures per package. SLH-DSA is specified but not implemented. Not intended for production use.
crypto_primitives: RSA (RSA/SHA512), ML-DSA-87, Ed448, ML-DSA-65, Ed25519, SHA512, SHA256, SHA1, MD5
key_management_model: OpenPGPv6 keys generated via Sequoia-PGP (sq) command line; supports primary key and signing subkey structure; keys stored in local RPM macro configuration.
architecture_type: Hybrid signature architecture (combining legacy RSA and PQC algorithms within RPMv6 format)
primary_platforms: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.1, Fedora (with configuration differences)
target_industries: Enterprise, IoT, Embedded systems, Software vendors
pqc_roadmap_details: Technology preview in RHEL 10.1; supports CNSA 2.0 approved ciphersuite mldsa87; SLH-DSA support planned but not implemented; future improvements to sequoia-sq for primary key signing capabilities expected.

product_brief: Integrated cybersecurity platform for enterprise supporting code signing, TLS, SSH, S/MIME, and certificate lifecycle management.
pqc_capability_description: The document discusses PQC as a future requirement under CNSA 2.0, mentioning LMS, XMSS, and ML-DSA as target algorithms for code signing. It states vendors should support PQC by 2025 with exclusive use by 2030. Garantir offers guidance on this transition but does not explicitly state that GaraTrust currently implements these PQC algorithms in production; it focuses on the need to migrate and audit current systems.
crypto_primitives: RSA, ECC (mentioned as legacy/vulnerable), LMS, XMSS, ML-DSA, Dilithium (mentioned in context of migration planning)
key_management_model: HSM-backed; keys stored in Hardware Security Modules with proxied access, centralized management, and granular per-key/per-user policies.
license: Proprietary (implied by "Commercial vendor" and "Request a Demo")
primary_platforms: Enterprise environments; specific OS or cloud platforms not listed.
target_industries: Software / SaaS, IoT, Manufacturing, Banking & Finance, Legal, Insurance, Healthcare, Government
regulatory_status: Mentions compliance with CNSA 2.0 (NSA standards) as a requirement for government vendors; no specific licenses or charters held by Garantir stated.
pqc_roadmap_details: NSA guidelines recommend supporting PQC algorithms by 2025 and exclusive use of quantum-safe signatures by 2030. Target algorithms include LMS, XMSS (stateful), and ML-DSA (stateless).
signature_schemes: RSA, ECC (legacy); LMS, XMSS, ML-DSA, Dilithium (planned/migration targets)

product_brief: Java Platform providing a quantum-resistant Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm (ML-DSA) implementation.
pqc_capability_description: Implements ML-DSA (FIPS 204) via KeyPairGenerator, Signature, and KeyFactory APIs in Release 24. Supports parameter sets ML-DSA-44, ML-DSA-65, and ML-DSA-87. Does not support Dilithium or Pre-Hash ML-DSA. Not yet integrated into JAR-file signing or TLS (javax.net.ssl) due to lack of standards.
crypto_primitives: ML-DSA, RSA, Diffie-Hellman, AES, Blowfish, ChaCha20-Poly1305, DES, DESede, EC, DSA, RC2, ARCFOUR, RSASSA-PSS, GCM, OAEP, PBE
key_management_model: Programmatic key management via KeyPairGenerator and KeyFactory APIs; supports PKCS #8 encoding for private keys and X.509 encoding for public keys.
fips_validated: No (References FIPS 204 standardization, but no FIPS 140 validation status stated for the implementation itself)
primary_platforms: All platforms to which the JDK is ported (including AArch64, RISC-V, s390x, PowerPC/AIX, BSD, Haiku, Mobile, etc.)
target_industries: Government (US government systems handling sensitive information), Enterprise
pqc_roadmap_details: ML-DSA implemented in Release 24. Support for JAR-file signing and TLS planned for future releases once standards exist. Pre-Hash ML-DSA may be implemented in a future release.
signature_schemes: ML-DSA (ML-DSA-44, ML-DSA-65, ML-DSA-87), RSA, DSA, ECDSA (implied via EC algorithm), RSASSA-PSS
